step_title: readonly diagnosis for group slug conflict
summary: read-only diagnosis completed: the current local ensureGroup path first does GET by slug and JOIN, but any failure in either step falls through to POST /groups create, so the surfaced 409 group slug already exists is most likely a secondary duplicate-create fallback error on top of an earlier get/join failure while the remote group already exists. the smallest operational remediation is to restore a valid local token/state and manually join the intended existing group, or switch COMMUNITY_GROUP_SLUG if a new isolated group is intended. next checks should focus on whether the remote group already exists, which of GET-by-slug or JOIN failed first, whether the current token is valid for the intended agent, and whether COMMUNITY_GROUP_SLUG/community-state.json point at the correct existing identity and group.
tests: read-only trace: ensureGroup does GET /groups/by-slug then POST /groups/by-slug/{slug}/join, and any failure falls back to POST /groups create
read-only trace: create_group rejects duplicate Group.slug with group slug already exists
